label: Service sheet
description: Help users check if they are ready to start a service. Users may arrive from a catalogue, chat, search, posters, or referrals.
purpose: Confirm the user has found the right service and prepare them to start.
use_when: At the start of a service that involves the user inputting information to get something.
Before an application form or multi-step journey.
do_not_use_when: The service is purely informational with no user action required.
The user has already started the service and is mid-journey.

variants.label: Service poster (offline)
variants.description: A physical or static version used in offline spaces. Much shorter content, no interaction. Must include a clear next step (QR code, URL, phone number, location).
use_when: Service must be accessible in physical locations without internet.

ux_considerations.text: Include service name, what will happen, what users will get, and any cost.
Provide a clear call-to-action button to start the service (e.g. 'Start now').
Provide support for users who cannot access online — include phone, text relay, or in-person alternatives.
Include a 'sign in' or 'continue journey' option if the user can resume an existing session.

technical_notes.text: Service sheet content should be machine-readable so agents can extract service prerequisites automatically.
